headline: Result of AGM- iShares plc 22nd July
Result of AGM
brief: Molten Ventures shareholders approve all 19 AGM resolutions with strong majorities, including director re-elections and 2026 Long Term Incentive Plan.
summary: All resolutions passed at Molten Ventures' 22 July 2026 AGM with overwhelming support. Annual Report & Accounts received 99.79% approval; Directors' Remuneration Report 99.00%; all eight directors re-elected with 88-99.7% support; PricewaterhouseCoopers reappointed auditor (99.94%); share allotment authority approved (98.83%); 2026 Long Term Incentive Plan adopted (97.92%). Special resolutions on share buybacks (99.99%) and pre-emption rights disapplied (93-98.6%) also passed. Voting by poll reflected strong shareholder consensus across governance matters.
retail_take: Routine AGM governance approval with no contentious issues. Strong voting margins on all resolutions, especially director re-elections, suggest healthy shareholder confidence in management and strategic direction. Non-event for market.

headline: DP World to expand with new Fujairah terminals
brief: DP World to develop two new terminals in Fujairah under 50-year concession, adding 2.5m TEU and 3.6m tonnes capacity.
summary: DP World has secured a 50-year concession with Fujairah Ports Authority to build Al Rugaylat container/multi-purpose terminal (2.5m TEU, 1.7m tonnes general cargo annually) and Dibba General Cargo terminal (3.6m tonnes annually). Construction will take 24-30 months. The development increases DP World's UAE container capacity from 19.4m to 22m TEU, with terminals integrated into the Jebel Ali ecosystem via inland logistics network. Projects positioned to handle Ultra Large Container Vessels on UAE's east coast.
retail_take: Capacity expansion addressing Jebel Ali high utilisation signals confidence in long-term regional trade growth and provides earnings uplift, though capex requirements and long construction timeline push benefits to 2028+.

headline: Correction: Trading Statement-Typographical Error
brief: Dialight issues corrected trading statement with typographical error fix; Q1 FY2027 results positive.
summary: Dialight PLC corrected a minor typographical error in CFO Mark Fryer's name from its Q1 trading update. The company delivered strong sales growth exceeding 3-5% guidance, gross margins surpassed 45% target, and achieved 15%+ return on sales in the quarter. The group moved to net cash position with further generation expected, prompting the board to upgrade full-year profit expectations ahead of prior guidance. Interim results for six months ending 30 September 2026 due 10 November 2026.
retail_take: The correction itself is administrative, but the underlying Q1 performance—exceeding guidance across sales, margins, and profitability with upgraded full-year outlook—is likely positive for the share price and demonstrates operational momentum.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Dialight CEO Steve Blair purchases 6,750 shares at 440p, increasing stake to 0.29%.
summary: Steve Blair, CEO of Dialight plc, purchased 6,750 ordinary shares at 440 pence per share on 22 July 2026 via the London Stock Exchange. Following this transaction, Blair now holds 118,479 ordinary shares, representing 0.29% of the company's issued share capital. The purchase represents an insider investment by the CEO in the LED lighting and opto-electronic components company.
retail_take: Routine director dealing disclosure. CEO share purchase may signal confidence but at modest stake level (0.29%); typical PDMR notification with no material strategic implications.

headline: Settlement of Litigation
brief: Tavistock Investments and Titan Wealth Services settle litigation between them; terms kept confidential.
summary: Tavistock Investments PLC and Titan Wealth Services Limited have reached a settlement agreement resolving all claims and counterclaims in ongoing legal proceedings involving their group companies, affiliates and directors. The specific terms of the settlement remain confidential and will not be disclosed publicly. No financial details or timeline have been announced.
retail_take: Resolution of litigation removes legal uncertainty and potential financial exposure, likely viewed positively as it provides closure without disclosed costs.

headline: Issue of Equity
brief: Bridgepoint Group issues 3.66m ordinary shares following ECP Sellers' exchange of OP Units under 2023 transaction structure.
summary: Bridgepoint is issuing 3,657,040 ordinary shares (£0.00005 each) following an exchange notice from Energy Capital Partners Sellers regarding OP Units granted during the October 2023 ECP acquisition. The shares rank pari passu with existing shares and admission to the LSE main market is expected 27 July 2026. This represents a partial exchange of the 185 million OP Units originally issued under the Up-C transaction structure approved by shareholders in October 2023.
retail_take: Routine equity issuance linked to pre-approved acquisition structure; represents modest shareholder dilution but reflects execution of known transaction terms with no material downside surprise.

headline: Post Stabilisation Notice - PRICOA FABN GBP 5yr

company: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Ltd
headline: Special Explanation on Asset Acquisition Progress
brief: Ming Yang Smart Energy delays shareholder meeting on Uniwatt Technology acquisition beyond 6-month regulatory deadline.
summary: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group is acquiring 100% equity of Uniwatt Technology Co., Ltd. through share issuance and cash payment with supporting funds. Due diligence work (audit, valuation, legal) remains incomplete and transaction details require further discussion. The company expects to miss the 6-month statutory deadline to convene a shareholders' meeting from the January 22, 2026 board resolution announcement. The transaction is classified as a related-party transaction, not a major asset restructuring, and remains subject to regulatory approvals.
retail_take: Delayed progress signals execution risks and regulatory complexity, potentially concerning investors awaiting deal closure. Market likely views this negatively as continued uncertainty, though the company reaffirms commitment to advance the transaction.

headline: Financial Results for the Quarter Ended 30 June 26
brief: United Bank Limited reports H1 2026 results with 33% interim dividend and announces three major strategic initiatives.
summary: UBL posted H1 2026 profit after tax of PKR 84.97bn (H1 2025: PKR 63.79bn), with earnings per share of 33.93 rupees. Board recommended 160% interim cash dividend of Rs 8/share for Q2 2026, in addition to prior Q1 dividend of Rs 8/share. Key announcements include: PKR 8bn investment in new agriculture-focused subsidiary; PKR 22bn equity investment in Khushhali Microfinance Bank (which had negative equity of Rs 16.15bn at end-2025); and PKR 10bn university contribution over three-to-five years with Bestway Foundation. New Company Secretary appointed: Shoukat Ali.
retail_take: Strong earnings growth and generous dividend payout signal shareholder-friendly capital management, but substantial equity injections into troubled microfinance partner and new ventures suggest management expects slower organic growth ahead and heightened exposure to agricultural sector risks.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Tate & Lyle shareholders approve all 22 AGM resolutions with strong majorities on 22 July 2026.
summary: All resolutions at Tate & Lyle's AGM passed decisively. Report and accounts, final dividend, and auditor reappointment (Ernst & Young) each secured ~98.8% support. Board re-elections ranged from 93.6-98.6% approval, with new director Heather Harding elected at 98.6%. Capital authorities including share allotment and buyback powers approved at 93-98.8%. The remuneration report received lower support at 85.1%, though still passed comfortably.
retail_take: Routine AGM outcome with routine governance votes; strong shareholder support indicates no significant governance concerns, though lower remuneration vote warrants monitoring.

headline: Post Stabilisation Notice - Unicaja EUR 7NC6 SNP
Results of Annual General Meeting
brief: Experian's AGM approves all resolutions with strong shareholder backing; board and auditors reconfirmed.
summary: All 17 resolutions passed at Experian's July 22 AGM with substantial majorities. Annual report received 99.59% approval; remuneration policy 90.58%; all nine directors reelected with 90-99% support. KPMG reappointed as auditor (99.07%). Shareholders granted authority to repurchase up to 89.7m shares at prices between 10 US cents and 105% of five-day average, expiring October 2027. Share capital consists of 943.97m ordinary shares, with 56.31m held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine disclosure with overwhelming shareholder support across governance matters. Board alignment and executive remuneration policies endorsed strongly. Share buyback authority renewal is standard corporate practice. No material concerns signalled.

headline: Update on Sales in USA
brief: Cizzle's lung cancer blood test now available for sale in Dallas-Fort Worth and San Antonio, Texas through CLIA-certified partner OmniHealth Diagnostics.
summary: Cizzle Bio Inc, the North American licensing partner of UK-listed Cizzle Biotechnology, has launched commercial sales of the CIZ1B Biomarker Blood Test for Lung Cancer in Dallas-Fort Worth and San Antonio metropolitan areas in Texas. The test completed laboratory validation and received CLIA approval at OmniHealth Diagnostics in June 2026. These high-population markets were selected for initial rollout due to large at-risk populations and strong physician networks, with expansion through additional CLIA-certified laboratory partners expected in coming months across the United States, Canada and the Caribbean.
retail_take: Material commercial milestone moving from validation to revenue-generating sales in large US markets, suggesting progress toward profitability; depends heavily on physician adoption rates and expansion execution to validate market opportunity.

headline: First Quarterly Interim Dividend
brief: HICL Infrastructure declares first quarterly interim dividend of 2.12p per share for FY2027.
summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC announces Q1 dividend of 2.12 pence per ordinary share for the financial year ending 31 March 2027. Ex-dividend date is 27 August 2026, with payment on 30 September 2026 to shareholders registered by 28 August 2026. Of this dividend, 74% is designated as an interest distribution for UK tax purposes. A Dividend Re-investment Plan is available for shareholders wishing to reinvest dividends.
retail_take: Routine dividend declaration from established infrastructure fund maintaining consistent distribution policy; likely well-received by income-focused investors but represents standard operational communication.

headline: Share Plan Purchase
brief: Investec plc and Investec Limited acquired 600,000 shares via Share Incentive Plans between 17-21 July 2026.
summary: The dual-listed Investec entities purchased shares on-market to satisfy Share Incentive Plan obligations to participants. Investec Limited acquired 300,000 shares at ZAR 135.25 average price (total ZAR 40.6m), while Investec plc acquired 300,000 shares at GBP 6.23 average price (total GBP 1.87m). Transactions occurred 17-21 July 2026 with prior regulatory clearance.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for employee incentive plans; demonstrates ongoing employee engagement but carries no significant strategic or valuation implications for shareholders.

headline: Post Stabilisation Notice - EEW, EUR, 3y Green
Release of Monies from Escrow in Cyprus
brief: DCI Advisors releases €2.90m from Cyprus escrow account; €9.65m in further receivables pending tax clearances.
summary: Following the sale of Aristo Developers interests announced in February and May 2025, DCI Advisors has settled liabilities and released €2.90m from a €3.20m escrow account in Cyprus. The company expects to receive €6.15m from the sale of ordinary shares in DCI Holdings Two Limited and €3.50m from Class A Preferred shares representing Venus Rock ownership, contingent on Cyprus tax authority clearances and tax settlements. The company has provisioned for estimated taxes in its valuation of these receivables.
retail_take: Positive signal: cash recovery from prior asset sale with material receivables pipeline, though realization dependent on tax clearance timing, creating execution risk for investors.

headline: Notice of AGM and posting of Annual Report
brief: Calnex Solutions announces AGM for 2 September 2026 with hybrid attendance option; recommends 0.68p final dividend.
summary: Calnex Solutions' Annual General Meeting will be held on 2 September 2026 at Oracle Campus, Linlithgow. Shareholders can attend in person or remotely via Zoom (pre-registration required by 19 August). The Board recommends a final dividend of 0.68p per ordinary share for the year ended 31 March 2026, payable 8 September 2026 to shareholders on the register as at 14 August 2026. Annual Report and proxy materials have been posted to shareholders and are available online.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of AGM and dividend timetable; provides no new operational or financial information beyond previously announced FY26 results released 26 May 2026.

headline: Block Admission
brief: Capricorn Energy admits 5.3m shares via block admission to support employee share schemes.
summary: Capricorn Energy has applied to the London Stock Exchange for block admission of 5,316,739 ordinary shares to satisfy employee share plan exercises and awards. Of these, 845,313 shares will be allotted to the employee benefit trust on 23 July 2026 for Long Term Incentive Plan vesting on 28 July. Following this allotment, total voting rights will be 71,403,652 ordinary shares. The remaining shares may be allotted later for future option exercises and award vestings.
retail_take: Routine disclosure for employee incentive plan fulfilment; standard practice for listed companies and non-material to equity holders given modest dilution context.

headline: Result of Tender Offer
brief: Redcentric completes £90m share buyback tender offer; 56.25m shares acquired at £1.60 per share.
summary: Redcentric repurchased the maximum 56.25m ordinary shares (35.3% of issued capital) at £1.60 per share through a tender offer that closed 21 July 2026, returning £90m to shareholders. Tendering demand totalled 150.3m shares; those tendering at or below their basic entitlement were accepted in full, with excess applicants receiving approximately 2.01% acceptance. The shares will be cancelled following Cavendish's exercise of a put option. Post-restructure, 103.07m new ordinary shares will be admitted to AIM on 29 July with updated SEDOL/ISIN; shareholder payments expected by 4 August.
retail_take: Completed capital return with strong oversubscription signals shareholder appetite; share consolidation reduces share count but £90m cash outlay warrants scrutiny of balance sheet impact and strategic rationale.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Jardine Matheson repurchased 12,000 shares at average price of US$65.12 on 22 July 2026; shares to be cancelled.
summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings completed a share repurchase on 22 July 2026, acquiring 12,000 ordinary shares at prices ranging from US$64.70 to US$65.37, with a weighted average price of US$65.1208. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, JMH's issued share capital comprises 294,037,976 ordinary shares with one vote each and holds no treasury shares.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; modest buyback scale suggests measured shareholder returns rather than aggressive consolidation. Market likely reads as neutral operational housekeeping.

headline: Block Listing Six Monthly Return
brief: Hill & Smith reports six-monthly block listing return for employee share schemes.
summary: Hill & Smith PLC filed its block listing six-monthly return for employee share schemes covering January-July 2026. The company issued/allotted 108,418 securities during the period, reducing the unallotted balance from 278,180 to 169,762. No increase to the block scheme limit was applied for during this reporting period.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of employee share scheme activity; indicates modest employee share plan participation but carries no material strategic or financial implications for investors.

headline: TR-1: Standard form-notification of major holdings
brief: Suntera Private Wealth (Jersey) Limited disclosed 9.98% voting stake in Playtech PLC as of 4 June 2026.
summary: Suntera Private Wealth (Jersey) Limited, acting as trustee of Playtech plc's Employee Benefit Trust (EBT), notified a major shareholding of 30,859,398 voting rights representing 9.977% of Playtech PLC. The threshold was crossed on 4 June 2026 but notification occurred on 21 July 2026. This represents a marginal decrease from the previous notification level of 10.078%.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of employee benefit trust holding; no material implications for shareholder value or control, as EBT holdings are typically non-voting for management purposes.

headline: Portfolio Update
brief: BlackRock American Income Trust posts 5.0% June NAV return, outperforming benchmark; AI-driven strategy capturing semiconductor and financial strength.
summary: For June 2026, BlackRock American Income Trust (BRAI) delivered 5.0% NAV return against 3.8% for Russell 1000 Value Index net of fees. The £162.7m trust maintains 5.1% net yield with quarterly dividends; share price at 273.00p trades at 1.3% premium to cum-income NAV of 269.55p. Portfolio benefited from AI infrastructure demand supporting semiconductors and financials; detracted by materials and energy weakness. One-year NAV performance stands at 38.0% versus 30.9% benchmark. Systematic AI-driven approach using big data analytics continues outperforming, with ongoing charges at 0.73%.
retail_take: Solid outperformance driven by AI/semiconductor positioning aligns with tech market momentum; above-benchmark returns and attractive income yield support the systematic strategy's credibility, likely viewed positively by income-focused investors.

headline: Issue of Equity
brief: Acuity RM settles £24,011 supplier debt via 3.2m share issuance at 0.75-0.755p.
summary: Acuity RM Group (AIM: ACRM) has issued 3,193,919 new ordinary shares to settle £24,011 of supplier invoices. Of these, 1,127,252 shares were priced at 0.7550p (April 2026 average closing price) and 2,066,667 at 0.7500p (June 2026 fundraise price). Admission to AIM trading expected 24 July 2026. Post-admission, total voting rights will be 308,737,151 shares.
retail_take: Minimal material impact—small debt settlement via scrip issuance at recent market prices; routine capital management with negligible dilution in context of £24k liability.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Cobas Asset Management reduces Currys holding from 3.83% to 2.91% of voting rights.
summary: Cobas Asset Management, SGIIC, S.A. (Madrid) crossed a notifiable threshold on 21 July 2026 by reducing its voting rights in Currys PLC from 3.825044% to 2.912762%, representing 31.8 million shares. The reduction falls below the 3% threshold, triggering this TR-1 disclosure. Shares are managed across multiple client portfolios with delegated voting rights to the investment manager.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a threshold crossing notification. The fund's partial exit is a modest position reduction that doesn't signal major sentiment change about the retailer.

headline: Director Dealing
brief: Hercules PLC Non-Executive Director Martin Tedham acquires 50,000 shares, raising stake to 13.06%.
summary: Martin Tedham, Non-Executive Director of Hercules plc (AIM: HERC), purchased 50,000 ordinary shares at 32.0 pence per share on 21 July 2026 through Wasdell Packaging Limited, a company he controls. Following this transaction, Tedham's beneficial holding increased to 10,520,047 shares, representing 13.06% of the company's issued share capital. The purchase was disclosed under EU Market Abuse Regulation requirements.
retail_take: Director share purchase signals confidence in company valuation at 32p; modest positive signal but routine disclosure with limited market-moving significance.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Hongkong Land repurchased 351,000 shares at average price of US$7.69 on 21 July 2026; shares to be cancelled.
summary: Hongkong Land Holdings completed a share repurchase of 351,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at prices ranging from US$7.62 to US$7.73 per share, with a weighted average price of US$7.6926. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Post-repurchase, the company's issued share capital comprises 2,132,746,626 ordinary shares with one vote each, and no treasury shares are held.
retail_take: Routine capital management action demonstrating shareholder-friendly capital allocation; modest repurchase size suggests maintenance programme rather than aggressive buyback. Market likely treats as neutral to slightly positive signal of confidence.

headline: Final Terms
brief: Xtrackers ETC PLC issues 6,200 platinum ETC securities hedged to EUR, maturing May 2080.
summary: Tranche 42 of Series 7 platinum ETC securities issued on 23 July 2026 with 6,200 units, bringing total Series 7 to 278,348 securities. Issue price EUR 29.75 per security; metal entitlement 0.020942283 fine troy ounces. Base fee 0.38% per annum; FX hedging fee 0.35% per annum. Securities secured by platinum held by JPMorgan Chase Bank and listed on Frankfurt Stock Exchange and Borsa Italiana. Estimated net proceeds EUR 184,433.
retail_take: Routine issuance of commodity-linked securities under existing programme; standard terms suggest operational continuation with no material market implications.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: First Seagull AS crosses notifiable threshold in Headlam Group PLC with 7.08% holding.
summary: Norwegian company First Seagull AS has acquired voting rights in Headlam Group PLC, crossing the notifiable threshold on 21 July 2026. The entity now holds 5,724,776 voting rights representing 7.0810% of the company's share capital. First Seagull AS stated it is not controlled by any natural person or legal entity and does not control other undertakings with interests in Headlam. The notification was filed on 22 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding notification. No indication of activist intent or strategic development; likely a neutral market read unless further context emerges.

company: Aberdeen UK Smaller Cos. Grwth Trst
headline: Director Declaration
brief: Non-executive director Stephen Russell to take additional role at BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust from November 2026.
summary: Stephen Russell, a non-executive director of Aberdeen UK Smaller Companies Growth Trust plc, will be appointed as a non-executive director of BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc, effective 2 November 2026. The appointment is disclosed under Listing Rule 6.4.9R requirements for director announcements of other publicly quoted directorships.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of director's appointment to another investment trust board; demonstrates confidence in Russell's governance expertise but no material operational impact on Aberdeen trust.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Imperial Brands repurchased 167,259 shares at average price of 2,810.85p under its £1.45bn buyback programme.
summary: Imperial Brands purchased 167,259 ordinary shares for cancellation on 21 July 2026 under its £1.45bn share repurchase programme announced in October 2025. The shares were bought at an average price of 2,810.85p, with prices ranging from 2,798p to 2,825p. Following settlement and cancellation, 766,956,628 ordinary shares remain in issue. Purchases were executed via Barclays Capital across multiple trading venues.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution supporting EPS accretion and capital management. Share repurchases at this price level suggest management confidence, though modest daily volumes indicate orderly execution rather than aggressive positioning.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: UBS Group AG crosses 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB Group Holdings PLC.
summary: UBS Group AG, through its investment bank and wealth management division, notified on 21 July 2026 that it crossed the 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB Group Holdings PLC on 20 July 2026. UBS now holds 4.03% of voting rights via direct share ownership (21,955,484 shares) and 0.09% through financial instruments including share substitution rights and basket swaps, totalling 4.12% of voting rights. The previous notification showed 4.11%, indicating a marginal increase from the prior 3.07% direct holding.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding notification; UBS's stake remains below 5% threshold for enhanced regulatory scrutiny, suggesting this is a passive investment position rather than an active control stake.

headline: Notice of Results
brief: Drax Group to host half-year results webcast on 30 July 2026 at 9:00am UK time.
summary: Drax Group PLC will present its half-year results 2026 via live webcast on Thursday 30 July 2026 at 09:00 UK time. The presentation will be accessible remotely with no pre-registration required, and a recording will be made available afterwards. Presentation slides will be available from 07:00 on the same date via the investor relations website.
retail_take: Routine regulatory announcement scheduling results presentation; no material substance disclosed; market awaits actual results on 30 July.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: UBS Group AG crosses 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB, triggering mandatory disclosure.
summary: UBS Group AG, through its subsidiary UBS AG, notified on 21 July 2026 that it crossed the 4% voting rights threshold in Permanent TSB Group Holdings PLC on 20 July 2026. UBS holds 21.96 million voting rights (4.03% of shares) plus 0.09% through financial instruments, totaling 4.12% voting rights. This represents an increase from the previous 4.11% position, with the stake held through a controlled chain: UBS Group AG > UBS AG > UBS Europe SE > UBS Switzerland AG.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding crossing regulatory threshold; no strategic change indicated as position remains near 4% level with minimal movement from prior notification.

headline: Admission to Trading (XS3391834812, SPUK 119)
brief: CIBC admits GBP 818,717 capital protected notes to LSE trading, 10-year maturity due July 2036.
summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (LEI: 2IGI19DL77OX0HC3ZE78) announced admission to trading on 22 July 2026 of GBP 818,717 Capital Protected Issuer Callable Fixed Rate Notes (XS3391834812, SPUK 119) due July 2036 on the London Stock Exchange main market. The notes were issued under CIBC's Structured Note Programme. Full details available in the Base Prospectus dated 23 January 2026 and Final Terms published 14 July 2026, accessible via the LSE regulatory information service and FCA's National Storage Mechanism.
retail_take: Routine capital markets issuance by a major bank; admission to trading is standard administrative process. Non-event for equity investors; relevant primarily to fixed income and structured product markets.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Estate of William Black entities reduce Plexus Holdings stake from 5.06% to 4.44% following share disposal.
summary: First Equity Limited, Armstrong Investments Limited, and Rath Dhu Limited, all controlled by the Estate of William Black, crossed a notification threshold on 20 July 2026 by reducing their combined voting rights in Plexus Holdings Plc from 8,670,000 shares (5.06%) to 7,670,000 shares (4.44%). The disposal was notified to the issuer on 21 July 2026. The stake is now held through Nortrust Nominees Limited.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder reducing stake below the 5% threshold through ordinary share disposal; likely a non-event absent broader strategic context.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: BH Macro repurchases 40,000 Sterling and 5,000 US Dollar shares to hold in treasury.
summary: BH Macro Limited purchased 40,000 Sterling shares at 4.1950p and 5,000 US Dollar shares at 4.3000 on 21 July 2026 via JP Morgan Securities. Shares will be held in treasury. Post-transaction, Sterling shares in issue total 300,073,775 (excluding 77,681,937 in treasury) and US Dollar shares total 23,572,477 (excluding 935,301 in treasury). Total voting rights now 459,337,749.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury; typical capital management activity for closed-ended funds. No material impact signaled; largely administrative disclosure of voting rights.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Paragon Banking Group appoints two Non-Executive Directors effective 1 October 2026; two nine-year directors depart.
summary: Paragon Banking Group appoints Andrew Stewart and Rebecca McNeil as Non-Executive Directors from 1 October 2026, following orderly succession planning. Stewart, Senior Partner at Kearney, brings financial services expertise in strategy, AI and transformation. McNeil, formerly CEO of Retail at Close Brothers (2020-2023) and COO of Business Banking at Barclays, brings commercial and SME banking experience. Departing directors Graeme Yorston and Barbara Ridpath resign 30 September 2026 after nine years' service.
retail_take: Routine board succession reflecting planned management transition; appointments strengthen technology and banking expertise while departures conclude long tenures—typical governance activity with no material financial implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: GCP Infrastructure repurchases 185,706 shares at 83.07p average price on 21 July 2026.
summary: GCP Infrastructure Investments repurchased 185,706 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 83.07p, with a range of 82.90p to 83.20p. The purchase was made through Canaccord Genuity under shareholder authority from the February 2026 AGM. Since the buyback programme launched in December 2024, the company has cumulatively purchased 80.1 million shares for treasury, reducing voting shares to 787.7 million.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity reflecting board confidence in valuation; demonstrates continuation of shareholder-approved buyback programme with no material implications for near-term investor sentiment.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: B&M passes all 20 AGM resolutions with strong majorities; shareholder dissent noted on acquisitions resolution.
summary: All resolutions at B&M's 21 July 2026 AGM passed overwhelmingly, with typical support above 99% for routine matters. However, resolution 19—authorising share allotment for acquisitions and capital investments—received significant opposition (21% against vs 79% for), prompting the Board to commit to shareholder engagement and disclosure within six months. Peter Pritchard was elected director alongside reappointment of existing board members; Nadia Shouraboura retired. KPMG LLP was reappointed auditor.
retail_take: Routine AGM outcome with material caveat: the 21% vote against acquisitions authority signals shareholder caution on M&A strategy, requiring Board response and potentially constraining expansion flexibility.

headline: Change in Major Shareholding
brief: Norges Bank reduces Spirax Group stake to 2.94% from 3.29% following share disposal on 17 July.
summary: Norway's sovereign wealth fund Norges Bank crossed a notification threshold on 17 July 2026 by reducing its holding in Spirax Group PLC (ISIN GB00BWFGQN14). The bank's voting rights fell to 2.935% from 3.288%, comprising 1.916m direct shares (2.596%) and 250,785 shares via financial instruments (0.340%). The transaction was completed in Oslo on 20 July and notified to the issuer two days later. This represents a partial exit from the company's cap table.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder reduction below the 3% threshold. Markets typically view sovereign fund rebalancing as non-event trading; no strategic signal implied about Spirax fundamentals.

headline: Pre-close trading update - Correction
brief: Wetherspoon issues profit warning; sales growth of 4.0-4.2% offset by cost pressures.
summary: JD Wetherspoon reported like-for-like sales growth of 4.0% over 12 weeks to 19 July 2025 and 4.2% year-to-date. The 793-managed-pub operator opened eight pubs and sold nine, adding 15 franchised units. However, Chairman Tim Martin warned profits will fall below market expectations due to marginally lower final-quarter sales combined with elevated costs in food, labour, repairs, energy and business rates. Year-end net debt anticipated at £720m, unchanged from prior year-end.
retail_take: Markets likely to react negatively to the profit warning despite solid sales growth, as cost inflation outpacing revenue gains signals margin compression in a challenging operating environment.

headline: Publication of supplementary admission particulars
brief: Wessex Water Services Finance PLC publishes supplementary admission particulars for its £5bn EMTN programme.
summary: Wessex Water Services Finance PLC has published supplementary admission particulars dated 21 July 2026, updating base particulars from 16 December 2025 for its £5,000,000,000 Euro Medium Term Note Programme. The notes are unconditionally and irrevocably guaranteed by Wessex Water Services Limited. The supplementary document is available on Wessex Water's corporate website.
retail_take: Routine regulatory filing updating documentation for an existing debt issuance programme; no material news or implications for investors.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: NCC Group execs purchase shares through employee plan at £1.43 each on 17 July.
summary: Three NCC Group PDMRs executed routine monthly share purchases under the UK Share Incentive Plan on 17 July 2026: CEO Mike Maddison, CFO Guy Ellis, and CMO Angela Brown each purchased 115 ordinary shares at £1.4307 per share via the London Stock Exchange. The transactions represent standard employee participation in the company's share scheme.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of exec share plan participation; demonstrates management confidence but represents standard compensation mechanics rather than material market signal. Non-event for investors.

headline: Director Declaration
brief: F&C Investment Trust director Stephen Russell appointed non-executive director of BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust from November 2026.
summary: Stephen Russell, a director of F&C Investment Trust PLC, has been appointed as a non-executive director of BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc, effective 2 November 2026. This disclosure is made pursuant to Listing Rule paragraph 6.4.9(2)R regarding director appointments. The appointment represents a standard regulatory notification of an external directorship.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a director's external appointment. Market unlikely to view this negatively as non-executive roles are common for experienced investment professionals.

headline: Quarterly Report and Appendix 5B
brief: 88 Energy advances Alaska exploration with upgraded South Prudhoe resources and secured drilling rig for 2027 campaign.
summary: 88 Energy's South Prudhoe project gross unrisked 2U prospective resources increased ~35% to 768.9 MMbbls (640.7 MMbbls net to 88E). Augusta-1 exploration well targeting three reservoirs (Ivishak, Kuparuk, Upper Schrader Bluff) with 133.7 MMbbls gross unrisked 2U resources is planned for Q1 2027 spud, contingent on funding and permitting. Nordic Rig-3 and Arctic-rated camp secured. Company held A$8.2 million cash at quarter-end; Namibia PEL 93 farm-in amended reducing capital exposure by ~US$15 million while securing unconditional 20% working interest.
retail_take: Material resource upgrade and operational progress on flagship Alaska project supports exploration narrative, though 2027 spud remains dependent on securing funding and farm-out completion—execution risk remains significant for near-term catalysts.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IG Group transfers 2.1m treasury shares to employee benefit trust for long-term incentive plan awards.
summary: On 21 July 2026, IG Group transferred 2,100,000 ordinary shares from treasury to its Employee Benefit Trust at nil consideration to satisfy awards under the Long Term Incentive Plan 2023 and Sustained Performance Plan. Following the transfer, issued share capital stands at 361.6m shares, with 28.9m in treasury and 332.6m carrying voting rights. The transfer represents routine equity incentive plan administration.
retail_take: Routine equity incentive plan execution with no material market implications. Share dilution is pre-existing via treasury reserves rather than new issuance.

headline: Update on Eagle Lake Project
brief: Gunsynd's 10% stake in Eagle Lake gold project shows promising Phase 1 results ahead of Q4 2026 drilling.
summary: Talon Resources (90% owner of Wedgetail Mining, holder of Eagle Lake project) completed Phase 1 exploration in Ontario, Canada. Channel sampling confirmed gold mineralisation across multiple targets with highlights including 1.85m @ 7.17g/t Au and 4.80m @ 4.47g/t Au at East Fornieri Bay, plus a new Moss Knoll target identified. A 1,000m maiden diamond drilling programme is planned for Q4 2026, with results to be integrated with machine learning targeting. Gunsynd holds the remaining 10% interest in the project.
retail_take: Early-stage exploration success de-risks the project and supports confidence in imminent drilling, likely viewed positively by junior exploration investors, though Gunsynd's minority stake limits upside leverage compared to Talon.

headline: Amendment and Extension of Unsecured Loan Notes
2026 Fieldwork Underway at Eleonore North
brief: GreenX Metals commences summer fieldwork at Eleonore North project in East Greenland targeting gold, tungsten and antimony.
summary: GreenX Metals has begun active exploration at its Eleonore North Project in East Greenland, focusing on gold, tungsten, and antimony mineralisation. A RIRGS specialist will evaluate the Noa Pluton gold-antimony prospect and untested targets. Bulk sampling of tungsten and antimony material at North and South Margeries deposits will support metallurgical sighter test work. Archive core samples are being assayed with results expected in coming months. Tungsten and antimony are EU and US-designated critical raw materials with global supply concentrated in China; the project hosts high-grade occurrences in a stable Western jurisdiction.
retail_take: Early-stage exploration progress at a frontier project with exposure to supply-constrained critical materials; positive strategic positioning but metallurgical results remain pending and drilling decisions dependent on sighter test work outcomes.

headline: Trading Update and Notice of Interim Results
brief: Skillcast reports H1 2026 revenues of £8.2m (+10%), with ARR up 14% to £14.5m and strong cash position.
summary: Skillcast Group PLC (AIM: SKL) reported H1 2026 revenues of £8.2m, up 10% year-on-year, driven by recurring subscription revenues which grew 15% to £7.4m and now represent 90% of total revenues. ARR increased 14% over 12 months to £14.5m and 5% since year-start. The Group maintains no debt with £13.6m cash (15p per share). Profit margins continue improving through operational gearing and AI productivity gains. Full interim results due 30 September 2026.
retail_take: Solid organic growth with improving margins and strong cash generation, though CEO notes political/economic uncertainty is slowing customer decision-making; likely read as positive execution within guidance but tempered by macro headwinds.

headline: Hg announces an investment in Street Group
brief: HgCapital Trust invests £7m in Street Group, a Manchester-based PropTech software platform for UK estate agents, at £200m+ valuation.
summary: HgCapital Trust PLC, via manager Hg, is investing approximately £7 million in Street Group alongside other Hg institutional clients through the Hg Mercury Fund. Street Group, founded by siblings Tom and Heather Staff, values the company at over £200 million. The business provides integrated software and AI tools for UK estate and letting agents, including its CRM platform Street.co.uk, Spectre prospecting tool, and AI-native Cortex product. The founders retain majority control and will continue leading operations. HgT's available liquid resources total £278 million (12% of NAV), with outstanding commitments reducing to £2.1 billion.
retail_take: Positive signal: deployment of capital into high-growth PropTech with AI focus aligns with Hg's stated strategy; demonstrates active investment activity and confidence in emerging AI applications, though represents modest capital deployment relative to HgT's size.

headline: Notice of Interim Results
brief: PHP to announce interim results for H1 2026 on 30 July with analyst presentation and Q&A.
summary: Primary Health Properties PLC, the UK's largest listed healthcare REIT following its 2025 merger with Assura, will release interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 on 30 July 2026. The company operates a £6 billion portfolio of 1,142 healthcare properties across the UK and Ireland, generating £342m annual rental income and serving over 10 million patients. A virtual presentation for analysts and investors will be held at 11:30am BST with webcast access and Q&A.
retail_take: Routine results announcement for a major healthcare infrastructure REIT with strong fundamentals; market likely neutral as this is standard disclosure, though H1 performance details may move sentiment once published.

headline: Portfolio news: CRI wins EU grant
brief: Frontier IP portfolio company Cambridge Raman Imaging wins €400k EU grant, signs China distribution deal, targets pharma quality control.
summary: Cambridge Raman Imaging (26.81% owned by Frontier IP) received a €400,000 European Innovation Council Transition grant for scaling its Raman imaging technology. CRI signed a distribution agreement with Hooke Instruments to enter the Chinese market and announced a strategic pivot toward pharmaceutical industrial quality control, where the global market is valued at $5 billion and forecast to reach $13 billion by 2033. The company is in discussions with two leading pharmaceutical groups for pilot testing.
retail_take: Non-dilutive EU funding and concrete pharma sector traction suggest CRI is progressing toward commercialisation with lower regulatory barriers than medical markets, potentially accelerating value realisation for Frontier IP's stake.

headline: First Siemens Turbines Arrive at Port of Houston
Transense appoints distributors for Translogik
brief: Transense appoints Hamaton and Tyresure as European distributors for Translogik tyre inspection products and TireTask software.
summary: Transense Technologies has appointed Hamaton Ltd and sister company Tyresure Ltd as authorised distributors for Translogik tyre inspection devices and TireTask digital management platform across Europe and the Middle East. Hamaton provides established European logistics infrastructure, regional warehousing and multilingual support for the TPMS brand. The appointment targets growing regulatory demand from mandatory TPMS fitment on commercial vehicles, Digital Product Passport requirements, and RFID-enabled tyre adoption. Transense retains direct sales capability and flexibility to appoint additional partners.
retail_take: Positive move expanding international reach through established regional partner with existing fleet and aftermarket relationships, capitalising on European regulatory tailwinds without capital-intensive direct operations.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Malaccan Holdings Concert Party increases stake to 29.61% via 612,000 share purchase at 6.86p per share.
summary: AMC V Leonardo SARL, associated with non-executive directors Przemysław Głębocki and Jakub Chechelski, purchased 612,000 ordinary shares at 6.86p on 21 July 2026. The Malaccan Holdings Ltd Concert Party now holds 279,425,426 shares, representing 29.61% of issued share capital. DP Poland operates 142 Domino's Pizza locations across Poland and Croatia, plus 70 Pizzeria 105 locations.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of PDMR shareholding change within concert party structure; incremental stake increase suggests ongoing confidence but represents non-material corporate event.

headline: Board Appointments
brief: Technology Minerals appoints Michel Cataldo as Executive Director for Defence and Resilience; Callum Sommerton joins as Non-Executive Director.
summary: Michel Cataldo, previously Non-Executive Director with 24 years British Army experience and defence sector expertise, becomes Executive Director heading Resilience and Defence effective 20 July 2026. Callum Sommerton, nominated under shareholder Jonathan Swann's relationship agreement (holding >15% post-placing), joins as Non-Executive Director. Sommerton brings CEO experience from Chill Brands Group and battery supply chain regulatory insight relevant to the Company's battery recycling and critical minerals strategy.
retail_take: Positive signal: promotions strengthen execution of Mantle strategy, with dedicated defence-focused leadership and board expertise in battery/regulatory compliance as company transitions from restructuring to commercialisation.

headline: CFO Resignation
brief: Potter & Moore CFO Qadeer Mohammed to resign, working six-month notice period ahead of AGM.
summary: Qadeer Mohammed, Chief Financial Officer of Potter & Moore PLC (AIM:PAM), has informed the Board of his intention to step down and will not seek re-election at the forthcoming Annual General Meeting. He will work a six-month notice period to ensure smooth transition. The Board will commence an external recruitment process to identify his successor. Chairman Paul Forster thanked Mohammed for his four-year contribution.
retail_take: Planned CFO departure with orderly transition process likely viewed as neutral; six-month notice minimises disruption risk, though investor focus will shift to successor calibre and any strategy implications.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Non-Executive Director Natalie-Jane Macdonald purchases 2,525 Pets at Home shares for £4,998.86.
summary: Natalie-Jane Macdonald, Non-Executive Director at Pets at Home Group Plc, purchased 2,525 ordinary shares at £1.9758 per share on 20 July 2026 via the London Stock Exchange, totalling £4,998.86. This is an initial notification of a director shareholding transaction, representing a modest personal investment by a board member.
retail_take: Routine disclosure; director's modest share purchase signals minor confidence but carries minimal market significance given small transaction size.

headline: Purchase of Shares
brief: ProService CEO Thomas Shorten purchases 639,466 shares across two days at average price of 2.2p.
summary: Thomas Shorten, Chief Executive Officer of ProService Building Services Marketplace plc (AIM: PRO), purchased 639,466 ordinary shares on 20-21 July 2026. On 20 July, he bought 363,962 shares at 2.32p average; on 21 July, 275,504 shares at 2.08p average. Total investment: £14,068. Shorten's beneficial holding now totals 1,329,654 shares, including those held by closely associated persons.
retail_take: Insider buying by the CEO at subdued prices suggests management confidence in value, though modest transaction size limits conviction signal. Market likely treats this as mildly positive sentiment indicator.

headline: Appointment of Chief Commercial Officer
brief: Boku appoints Peter Klein as Chief Commercial Officer to drive merchant growth and expand its local payments network.
summary: Boku Inc (AIM: BOKU) has appointed Peter Klein as Chief Commercial Officer effective 22 July 2026. Klein brings 25+ years' experience in payments and fintech, most recently from Mastercard where he built the global Account-to-Account and Cross-Border Payments business. He will lead Boku's commercial organisation and go-to-market function, reporting to CEO Stuart Neal. The appointment supports growth ambitions as local payment methods are forecast to account for 60%+ of transactions in a $10.6trn global e-commerce market by 2028.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively as Klein's heavyweight Mastercard and payments sector pedigree signals serious commercial execution capability to capitalise on the high-growth local payments opportunity Boku targets.

headline: Cu-Ag Channel Results Confirm Scale of Monolith
brief: GSC's Monolith target confirms broad Cu-Ag mineralisation over 700x400m, positioning it as priority drill prospect.
summary: Great Southern Copper reported channel sampling results from 20 traverses (409m, 214 samples) at Monolith Target, Cerro Negro Prospect, Chile. Key intervals include 56m @ 28.5 g/t Ag & 1,745 ppm Cu, and 78m @ 10.1 g/t Ag & 736 ppm Cu. Results define Cu-Ag mineralisation across 700x400m in altered dacite dome, 400m from the Mostaza discovery. Surface oxidation/leaching suggests higher grades at depth. Company plans geophysical surveys and scout drilling to test the prospect.
retail_take: Positive indicator of exploration upside; broad surface anomalies and proximity to Mostaza deposit enhance prospectivity, though grades remain surface-level and drill confirmation is needed before major value creation.

headline: Full Year Results
brief: FRP Advisory delivered 7th consecutive year of profit and revenue growth to £177m, up 16%, with 12% EBITDA rise.
summary: Revenue grew 16% to £177.0m (FY2025: £152.2m) comprising 10% organic and 6% inorganic growth. Adjusted underlying EBITDA rose 12% to £46.1m. Reported profit before tax increased 13% to £35.5m. The firm maintained strong market positions, ranking 16th in UK M&A (up from 19th) and 14% market share in administrations. Net cash stood at £26.2m with no external borrowings. Total dividends increased 7% to 5.8p. Post-year-end, the Group secured £50m committed credit facilities plus £45m accordion capacity through June 2029.
retail_take: Consistent execution and market share gains in resilient advisory services amid economic uncertainty likely appeal to growth-focused investors, though working capital deployment and reduced net cash warrant monitoring.

headline: New European Automotive Program Award
brief: Seeing Machines secures European automotive DMS/OMS program worth ~US$5m lifetime revenue, integrating into rear-view mirrors from 2028.
summary: Seeing Machines has won a Driver & Occupant Monitoring System program with a European automotive OEM through an existing Tier 1 customer. The camera-based technology will integrate into rear-view mirror solutions across multiple vehicle platforms including electric vehicles, with production starting in 2028. The award is expected to generate approximately US$5 million in lifetime revenue and comes as EU General Safety Regulation requirements for driver monitoring take effect, positioning the company to benefit from industry transition toward camera-based in-cabin safety systems.
retail_take: Positive signal reinforcing Seeing Machines' market position and technology leadership; regulatory tailwinds and scalable rear-view mirror architecture reduce deployment barriers, supporting pipeline growth and revenue visibility into 2028.

headline: Departure of Directors or Certain Officers
Notice of Interim Results 2026
brief: Secure Trust Bank to release H1 2026 interim results on 13 August with investor presentations scheduled.
summary: Secure Trust Bank PLC will announce interim results for the period ending 30 June 2026 on Thursday 13 August 2026 at 7:00am. CEO Ian Corfield and CFO Rachel Lawrence will host a 10:00am webcast presentation covering half-year trading performance and strategic progress, with an additional shortened presentation via Investor Meet Company at 4:30pm the same day. Both presentations are open to existing and prospective shareholders.
retail_take: Routine announcement of interim results release date and investor meeting arrangements; no material disclosures provided. Market will await actual results on 13 August for substantive content.

headline: Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
brief: NWF Group to announce full year results for year ended 31 May 2026 on 28 July with analyst presentation and investor webinar.
summary: NWF Group plc will release full year results for the year ended 31 May 2026 on Tuesday 28 July 2026. An analyst presentation video will be available at 8:00am hosted by CEO Chris Belsham and CFO Katie Shortland. A live investor presentation via Investor Meet Company will follow at 11:00am, open to existing and potential shareholders with pre-submission of questions available until 27 July at 9:00am.
retail_take: Routine regulatory announcement of results release date and presentation arrangements; no material information disclosed; market will focus on actual results content when published.

headline: Follow on Satellite Orders
brief: EnSilica secures €1.1m follow-on satellite chip orders from European customer for ENS92040 beamformer ASSP.
summary: EnSilica announced €1.1 million in follow-on orders from a European satellite communications customer for its ENS92040 digital beamformer chip, comprising €350,000 for samples and €750,000 for product enhancements. The same customer previously ordered €2.5 million of the ASSP in August 2023. The ENS92040, developed with €5 million ESA funding, targets next-generation LEO, MEO and GEO satellite broadband terminals. High-volume production is anticipated from 2029 as new satellite constellations launch, with the satellite user terminal market expected to grow to over $3 billion by 2030.
retail_take: Repeat orders validate commercial traction for EnSilica's flagship satellite product and confirm market demand, though material revenue remains years away pending 2029 constellation deployments. Likely read as incrementally positive progression.

company: GCP Asset Backed Income Fund Ltd
headline: Fourth Compulsory Redemption
brief: GCP Asset Backed Income Fund announces fourth £45m compulsory redemption at 68.64p per share, 38.14% redemption ratio.
summary: GABI is executing its capital return strategy following a May 2024 discontinuation vote. The fourth compulsory redemption of £45.0 million will occur at 68.63875 pence per share, representing the 30 June 2026 NAV less declared dividends. Approximately 65.56 million shares (38.14% redemption ratio) will be cancelled on 31 July 2026, with payments by 14 August. To date, four distributions totalling £266.6 million have been returned to shareholders, reducing share capital by 61.11%.
retail_take: Positive for remaining shareholders as it reflects disciplined capital return during orderly wind-down; demonstrates management executing stated strategy with material progress on shareholder distributions.

headline: Exercise of Share Options
brief: First Class Metals CEO Marc Sale exercises 795,000 share options at 2.0p per share, investing £15,900.
summary: Marc Sale, Executive Director and CEO of First Class Metals PLC (LSE: FCM), exercised 795,000 share options on 21 July 2026, awarded to him on 17 July 2022 at an exercise price of 2.0 pence per ordinary share, resulting in aggregate cost of £15,900. The 795,000 new ordinary shares will be admitted to trading on 27 July 2026. Following issuance, the company's total voting rights will be 425,071,349 ordinary shares.
retail_take: Executive share option exercise signals management confidence but modest in scale relative to typical CEO holdings; routine PDMR disclosure with no material implications for shareholders.

headline: Director share purchase
brief: Non-Executive Director Zak McMurray purchases 39,840 shares at 251p, increasing his holding to 0.38% of issued capital.
summary: On 21 July 2026, Zak McMurray, Non-Executive Director of One Health Group PLC (AIM: OHGR), purchased 39,840 ordinary shares of 0.5p each at 251p per share via the London Stock Exchange. His total beneficial interest is now 53,173 shares, representing approximately 0.38% of the company's issued share capital. One Health is a profitable, dividend-paying NHS surgical provider treating ~19,000 patients annually across five regions, with FY26 revenue of £31.6m (+11%) and adjusted EBITDA of £2.58m (+28%).
retail_take: Director buying into own company signals confidence in growth strategy and recent strong FY26 results; positive signal but immaterial shareholding size and routine insider purchase mean limited market impact.

headline: Notification of Major Holdings
brief: Roaring Waters Capital notifies 6.55% stake acquisition in Poolbeg Pharma PLC.
summary: Roaring Waters Capital, Dublin-based entity, crossed a major shareholding threshold in Poolbeg Pharma PLC on 21 July 2026. The investor now holds 50,000,000 voting rights representing 6.55% of the company's voting capital, up from a previous position of 5.74%. The notification was filed on the same date the threshold was crossed.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of shareholding above notifiable threshold; no material transaction context provided, suggesting routine accumulation rather than strategic announcement.

headline: Shareholder Presentation via Investor Meet Company
brief: Sintana Energy to hold shareholder presentation via Investor Meet Company on August 6, 2026 following AGM.
summary: Oil and gas exploration company Sintana Energy (TSX-V: SEI, AIM: SEI, OTCQX: SEUSF) will host a live management update presentation on August 6, 2026 at 11:00 EDT/16:00 BST featuring CEO Robert Bose and President Eytan Uliel. The presentation follows the company's Annual General Meeting and is designed to provide existing and potential shareholders unable to attend the AGM with direct access to management. Investors can submit questions pre-event or during the live session via the Investor Meet Company platform.
retail_take: Routine investor engagement activity; non-event announcement providing no material operational or financial updates. Demonstrates standard shareholder communication practices for a geographically dispersed investor base.

headline: Grant of Awards under Long Term Incentive Plan
brief: Tungsten West grants LTIP options to three directors totalling 143.7m shares at 3p and 18p exercise prices.
summary: Tungsten West PLC granted conditional options to directors under its shareholder-approved LTIP on 21 July 2026. CEO Jeffery Court received 75.7m awards (60.5m at 3p, 15.1m at 18p); CFO Philip Povey received 37.8m awards (30.3m at 3p, 7.6m at 18p); Non-Executive Chairman Stephen Harrison received 30.3m awards at 3p. Approximately 20% of CEO and CFO awards carry 'stretch' performance targets significantly exceeding base case assumptions. Options lapse by 15 July 2041 unless earlier vesting conditions are met.
retail_take: Routine director remuneration disclosure aligned with shareholder-approved plan. Options vesting tied to production milestones for tungsten/tin mine restart; material only if Hemerdon project faces delays or abandonment.

headline: Notification of H1 2026 Trading Update
brief: Gem Diamonds to release H1 2026 trading update on 28 July covering January-June period.
summary: Gem Diamonds Limited will publish its half-year 2026 trading update on 28 July 2026 for the six-month period ending 30 June 2026. The company, which owns 70% of the Letšeng mine in Lesotho—the world's highest dollar per carat kimberlite diamond mine—continues focus on large, exceptional white diamonds. In June 2025, Gem Diamonds relinquished its Ghaghoo mining license in Botswana with no further obligations.
retail_take: Routine disclosure notification of upcoming results. Market will await actual H1 figures on 28 July to assess operational performance and diamond pricing trends in current market conditions.

headline: Notice of Results & Investor Presentation
brief: Zotefoams to report H1 2026 interim results on 5 August with analyst presentation and investor roadshow.
summary: Zotefoams PLC, a world leader in high-performance foams, will announce interim results for the six months to 30 June 2026 on 5 August 2026. An analyst meeting via Zoom begins at 9:30am BST. A separate investor presentation via Investor Meet Company platform follows at 1:30pm BST, featuring Group CEO Ronan Cox and Group CFO Nick Wright, open to existing and potential shareholders. Investors can pre-submit questions until 4 August 9:00am BST.
retail_take: Routine trading update announcement with structured investor engagement; market will focus on actual H1 results rather than this procedural notice.

headline: Thermo Fisher CDMO selected for Kapiglucagon pro
brief: ImmuPharma selects Thermo Fisher as CDMO partner for Kapiglucagon Type 1 diabetes program.
summary: ImmuPharma has appointed Thermo Fisher Scientific's Patheon pharma services as drug product CDMO for its Kapiglucagon glucagon prodrug program following a competitive tender. This CMC milestone supports progression toward IND submission and first-in-human studies. The selection follows Bachem AG's appointment as API manufacturing partner. ImmuPharma is evaluating a 505(b)(2) regulatory pathway in the US, potentially reducing development timeline and costs versus a full NDA by leveraging existing glucagon data.
retail_take: De-risks development pathway by securing established manufacturing partner and pursuing accelerated 505(b)(2) approval route; positive signal on program momentum, though execution risk remains pre-clinical.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Venture Life repurchases 190,000 shares at 70p per share under existing buyback programme.
summary: Venture Life Group PLC purchased 190,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at 70 pence per share via Cavendish Capital Markets, to be held as treasury shares. Following the repurchase, the company has 128.86 million shares in issue, with 7.95 million held in treasury, reducing voting rights to 120.91 million shares. The buyback was conducted under the share repurchase programme announced 30 September 2025.
retail_take: Routine capital management action; modest buyback at consistent price signals orderly execution but provides limited insight into management confidence or strategic direction for this healthcare products company.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: AEP Plantations bought 45,977 shares at 164-174p on 21 July under its buyback programme.
summary: AEP Plantations purchased 45,977 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at prices ranging from 164p to 174p, with a volume-weighted average price of 167.32p, through Cavendish Capital Markets. Since the buyback programme's announcement on 6 July, the company has acquired 580,292 shares held in treasury. Following this purchase, 383,013,188 voting shares remain in issue, with 16,749,532 shares held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within announced programme parameters; ongoing capital return activity with no material news flow or strategic implications for shareholders.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Science Group PLC repurchased 10,000 shares at 585p per share on 21 July 2026.
summary: Science Group plc purchased 10,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at 585 pence per share through Panmure Liberum Limited, representing 0.02% of voting rights. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, 40,662,593 shares remain in issue (excluding 5,523,281 treasury shares), with the company now holding approximately 12% of issued shares in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback continuation; modest capital allocation signaling management confidence in valuation, though immaterial scale suggests incremental rather than transformational capital deployment.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pantheon International repurchased 295,189 shares at 388-390.5p on 21 July; plans cancellation.
summary: Pantheon International PLC purchased 295,189 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities at a weighted average price of 389.66 pence per share, spending approximately £1.15m. The shares will be cancelled. Following the transaction, 393,963,764 ordinary shares remain in issue with total voting rights of 393,963,764.
retail_take: Share buyback with cancellation typically signals management confidence in valuation and returns capital to remaining shareholders; modest buyback size suggests measured capital allocation approach.

headline: High-Grade Gold Confirmed Across Multiple Targets
brief: Talon Resources confirms high-grade gold across Eagle Lake targets; maiden drilling planned Q4 2026.
summary: Talon Resources' Phase 1 exploration at Eagle Lake, Ontario returned strong gold results including 1.85m @ 7.17g/t Au and 4.80m @ 4.47g/t Au at East Fornieri Bay, plus 32.90g/t Au grab sample at Parker Shear. A new Moss Knoll target was identified, with 8.00m @ 0.67g/t Au. Results will be integrated with historical data and machine learning targeting to refine drill targets for a planned 1,000m maiden drilling programme in Q4 2026. The project covers 1,986 hectares with Talon holding a 90% interest.
retail_take: Early-stage exploration success with visible gold confirmation across multiple targets strengthens pre-drill confidence; Q4 drilling will provide first systematic test of discovery potential, likely supporting positive sentiment for exploration-stage investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Greencoat Renewables purchased 271,109 shares for cancellation as part of its March 2026 buyback programme.
summary: On 21 July 2026, Greencoat Renewables repurchased 271,109 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin at prices ranging from €0.7520 to €0.7720 per share, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7634. The shares will be cancelled. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,082,834,285 shares in issue. The transaction forms part of the buyback programme announced 5 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically signal management confidence but are non-events absent specific context around valuation or strategic initiatives.

headline: Second Oil Train Boosts Brent-Linked Liquid Output
brief: Energean completes second oil train on FPSO, boosting liquids capacity to 31 kbbl/d and increasing Brent-linked revenues.
summary: Energean PLC successfully commissioned its second oil train on the Energean Power FPSO on 13 July 2026, increasing total liquids processing capacity from 18 kbbl/d to 31 kbbl/d. Production was tested at rates up to 21 kbbl/d, with further testing expected in August 2026. H1 2026 Israeli liquids production averaged 10 kbbl/d (13 kbbl/d excluding a 41-day geopolitical shutdown); H2 2026 guidance projects 17-21 kbbl/d average, strengthening the company's position as Israel's largest liquids producer with higher Brent-linked revenue exposure.
retail_take: Positive operationally; capacity expansion and higher commodity-linked revenues should support earnings growth, though execution risk remains on further testing and geopolitical sensitivities persist.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gaming Realms repurchased 29,094 shares at 29.90-30.50p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Gaming Realms PLC purchased 29,094 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 via Peel Hunt at prices ranging from 29.90p to 30.50p, with a VWAP of 30.13p. The shares will be held in treasury. The company now holds 24,393,381 treasury shares with 271,872,633 shares in issue, representing total voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within an announced programme; supports capital management but signals no major strategic developments or material news.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Grafton Group purchases 60,000 shares at £9.41 average price as part of £25m buyback programme.
summary: On 21 July 2026, Grafton Group plc repurchased 60,000 ordinary shares at a volume-weighted average price of £9.4124 through Goodbody Stockbrokers on the London Stock Exchange. The shares are being cancelled as part of the £25m buyback programme announced 30 June 2026. To date, 959,172 shares have been purchased in aggregate through Goodbody and Deutsche Bank since programme commencement.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution; buyback in progress as planned. No material change in direction or strategic significance—standard shareholder return mechanism.

headline: Exercise of Warrants and Total Voting Rights
brief: RentGuarantor exercises 1.08m warrants, raising £189k; share count rises to 149.9m.
summary: RentGuarantor Holdings (AIM: RGG) received notice of warrant exercise for 1,080,000 ordinary shares at 17.5p per share, generating approximately £189,000 in gross proceeds. Following admission to AIM on 23 July 2026, total issued ordinary share capital will be 149,872,459 shares with one voting right each. The company holds no treasury shares.
retail_take: Routine warrant exercise with modest capital raise; shareholder dilution offset by cash injection; non-event for market.

headline: Trading Update
brief: Hardide trading update shows strong momentum with FY revenues tracking ahead of expectations; £4.5m capacity investment approved.
summary: Q3 revenues reached £4.1m, bringing nine-month total to £8.9m, ahead of prior guidance. Full-year performance now expected materially ahead of previous expectations (prior guidance: £13.4m revenues, £4.3m EBITDA). Board now confident of doubling revenues in current financial year and more than doubling again within 2-3 years. £4.5m capital investment approved for three new coating reactors operational in latter part of 2027. Company secured ~50% of anticipated 2027 tungsten gas requirements, mitigating input cost inflation.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum and upgraded guidance with visible investment backing growth ambitions should support positive sentiment, though execution risk on capacity delivery and Middle East/semi-conductor expansion remains.

headline: Trading update for the six months to 30 June 2026
brief: SigmaRoc posts strong H1 with 11.3% EBITDA growth and improved margins; maintains full-year guidance.
summary: SigmaRoc delivered H1 2026 revenue of £523.1m (+2.5% YoY) with underlying EBITDA of £131.2m (+11.3%), driving EBITDA margins up 200bps to 25.1%. Core volumes rose 1% in first increase in three years, with strong pricing offsetting modest revenue growth. EPS grew 12.2% to 5.23p. Covenant leverage improved to 1.66x from 2.04x. The group secured €825m investment-grade refinancing facility plus €300m accordion, and obtained permits for 64m tonnes additional limestone at Swedish Klinthagen operation. Board confident in delivering full-year consensus expectations.
retail_take: Solid operational delivery with margin expansion and deleveraging signals underlying business resilience; refinancing and permitting gains support growth strategy, likely viewed positively by market despite modest top-line growth.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IWG repurchased 153,656 shares on 21 July as part of ongoing buyback programme; 40.3m shares bought since December 2025.
summary: International Workplace Group plc purchased 153,656 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at volume-weighted average price of 1.9462 GBP across multiple venues (LSE, CBOE, Turquoise, Aquis). Since programme launch 31 December 2025, the company has acquired 40,316,339 shares. Post-purchase, 953,972,226 shares remain in issue excluding treasury stock. Shares will be cancelled or held in treasury per shareholder authority from May 2026 AGM.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within disclosed authority. Share buybacks can support EPS but market reaction depends on valuation views and broader company fundamentals not addressed here.

headline: Second quarter production report
brief: Fresnillo reports Q2 production with mixed results; gold up sequentially but silver down year-on-year; maintains full-year guidance.
summary: Fresnillo delivered Q2 attributable silver production of 10.9 moz, down 12.6% year-on-year due to planned Silverstream agreement end and lower grades at Saucito and Juanicipio. Gold production of 154.8 koz increased 13.8% sequentially but fell 1.9% year-on-year. First-half gold production of 290.9 koz decreased 7.3% year-on-year, primarily from lower ore grades and reduced processing at Herradura. Company maintains 2026 guidance: silver 42.0-46.5 moz, gold 500-550 koz. Interim results announced 4 August 2026.
retail_take: Mixed operational performance with expected silver headwinds offset by gold resilience and maintained guidance signals management confidence, though year-on-year declines reflect challenging grade environment at key assets.

headline: Announcement of Tender Price
brief: RIT Capital Partners announces tender offer price of £26.8515 per share, equivalent to 85% of NAV.
summary: RIT Capital Partners has set the tender price at £26.8515 per share for its £300 million share buyback programme announced in July 2026. This represents 85% of the preliminary diluted NAV per share as at 30 June 2026. The company will repurchase a maximum of 11,172,560 shares, with all participating shareholders having 8.21% of their tendered shares satisfied in full.
retail_take: Discount to NAV of 15% is standard for tender offers; buyback signals capital management but discount reflects execution costs and uncertainty, likely viewed as neutral by markets.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Experian repurchases 317,396 shares at 2,694p average price; shares to be cancelled.
summary: On 21 July 2026, Experian purchased 317,396 of its own shares on the London Stock Exchange through BofA Securities Europe SA as part of its June 2026 share repurchase programme. Shares were bought at prices ranging from 2,668p to 2,700p, with a weighted average price of 2,693.81p. All purchased shares will be cancelled. Weekly transaction updates will be published on Experian's website.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution reducing share count; signals management confidence in valuation but is non-event for investors absent broader programme context or strategic announcements.

headline: Trading Statement
brief: Dialight exceeds Q1 expectations with strong sales growth, gross margins above 45%, and upgraded profit forecast for FY2027.
summary: Dialight delivered Q1 sales growth significantly above its 3-5% guidance and achieved gross margins exceeding 45%, driving underlying profitability above the newly upgraded 15% return-on-sales target. The LED lighting company, which reported strong backlog in June 2026, has moved to a net cash position with expected further cash generation. Based on first quarter performance and current outlook, the board believes full-year profit for the year ending 31 March 2027 will exceed previous expectations.
retail_take: Strong operational delivery with raised guidance suggests market confidence in demand and execution; net cash position and margin improvement signal operational leverage, likely supporting a positive market reception.

headline: Resignation of Portfolio Manager
brief: Portfolio manager George Ensor resigns from River UK Micro Cap Ltd; will remain in role during transition.
summary: George Ensor, portfolio manager for River UK Micro Cap Limited, has resigned from his position. He will continue managing the portfolio while the Board assesses options for a successor or alternative management structure. The Board is prioritising orderly transition and continuity of investment strategy, with further updates to follow.
retail_take: Key person risk materialises but managed exit is orderly; market likely views negatively absent clarity on replacement quality and timeline.

headline: Trading Update - Melia ceases operations in Cuba
brief: Melia Hotels terminates Cuba operations effective 24 July 2026, impacting Ceiba's 5 hotels following US sanctions expansion.
summary: Melia Hotels International terminated all hotel management and commercialization services in Cuba effective 24 July 2026, directly affecting Ceiba's 5 hotels operated through Cuban joint ventures Miramar and TosCuba. The decision follows US Executive Order 14404 (1 May 2026) which expanded sanctions targeting foreign entities supporting Cuba's government, specifically designating GAESA and the Ministry of Tourism. Ceiba states occupancy has already deteriorated severely from reduced airlift, so additional 2026 income impact is expected to be minimal. Management is analyzing options including potential operational suspension, capital expenditure projects, and strategic search for alternative operators.
retail_take: Negative read: material disruption to Ceiba's core Cuba hotel operations with uncertain recovery timeline and operational/financial risks, though management downplays near-term earnings impact given already-weak occupancy.

headline: Half Year Trading Update
brief: NAHL reports H1 2026 revenues of £19.7m (+3%), underlying operating profit up 8% to £3.4m, net debt cut 68% to £1.0m.
summary: Consumer Legal Services division grew revenues 2% to £11.3m with profits up 52% to £1.2m, driven by higher National Accident Law settlements (£5.7m cash). Critical Care revenues rose 3% to £8.5m with strong expert witness services (+11%), though case management services declined 8%. Free cash flow surged 42% to £2.1m, reducing net debt from £3.2m to £1.0m. Board expects H2 2026 settlement volumes to decline materially due to lower prior enquiry placements, moderately increasing net debt.
retail_take: Solid H1 performance with strong cash generation and debt reduction offsets guidance for weaker H2 due to anticipated lower settlements; board exploring strategic options to accelerate shareholder value.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IHG repurchased 1,000 shares at average price of $155.39 on 21 July for cancellation.
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $153.45 to $156.40, with a volume-weighted average of $155.3931. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, IHG has 148.6 million shares in issue excluding treasury stock. The purchase was authorised by shareholders at the May 2025 AGM and follows instructions issued in February 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under existing authority; modest scale suggests ongoing capital management rather than significant shareholder returns programme.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Plus500 repurchased 9,475 shares at 4,036-4,108p as part of its buyback programme.
summary: On 21 July 2026, Plus500 purchased 9,475 ordinary shares through Panmure Liberum at prices ranging from 4,036p to 4,108p, with a volume-weighted average price of 4,079.03p. Following this transaction, 68,951,512 shares remain in issue (excluding 45,936,865 treasury shares). The repurchased shares will be held in treasury. This continues the buyback programme announced 9 February 2026 and commenced 16 February 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity with immaterial share count reduction; no material implications for investors but demonstrates continued capital return discipline.

headline: Subscription & GMI Loan Update
